sections.paragraph:
ἄμαχος, ον act. (as X. et al.; Jos., Ant. 15, 115) peaceable (so Kaibel 387, 6 ἄ. ἐβίωσα μετὰ φίλων; IKosPH 325, 9. The adv. ἀμάχως Sir 19:6 v.l.) of Christians gener. Tit 3:2; of congregational overseers 1 Ti 3:3.—M-M. TW.

act. act. = active

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

IKosPH IKosPH = The Inscriptions of Cos, ed. WPaton/EHicks—List 3

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

gener. gener. = generally

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 52
